Haruaki was the son of [[Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[Keichō]] 1 ([[1596]]) – [[Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[1614]]). During the [[Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[Genna]] 1 ([[1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[Genna]] 4 ([[1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[Kan'ei]] 3 ([[1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[Kan'ei]] era ([[1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[Keian]] 3 ([[1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hida-Takayama lords]] [[Category:Edo-period tea masters]] [[Category:Edo-period poets]] [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:Kanamori clan]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanamori Shigetoshi}}

Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Haruaki was the son of [[Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[Keichō]] 1 ([[1596]]) – [[Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[1614]]). During the [[Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[Genna]] 1 ([[1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[Genna]] 4 ([[1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[Kan'ei]] 3 ([[1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[Kan'ei]] era ([[1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[Keian]] 3 ([[1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hida-Takayama lords]] [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:Kanamori clan]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:江戸時代の歌人]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]]

During the famine of the [[Kan'ei]] era ([[1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[Keian]] 3 ([[1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hida-Takayama lords]] [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:Kanamori clan]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]] [[Category:飛騨高山藩主]]

{{Citations missing|date=April 2024}} {{Infobox samurai | name=Kanamori Shigeyori | image=Kanamori Shigeyori.jpg|200px | image_size=200px | caption=Owned by Ryōgen-in | period=[[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]] - [[Edo period]] early | birth=[[Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) | death=[[Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]]) | other_names=(Childhood name) Sahei | posthumous_name=Shinryūinden Zuion Sōshō Daikoji | burial=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku]], Murasakino, [[Daitoku-ji]] | court_Rank=[[Junior Fifth Rank|Jushii]]; [[Nagato Province|Nagato no Kami]]; [[Izumo Province|Izumo no Kami]] | shogunate=[[Edo shogunate]] | master=[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada|Hidetada]] → [[Tokugawa Iemitsu|Iemitsu]] | domain=[[Hida-Takayama Domain]] lord | clan=[[Kanamori clan]] | parents=Father: [[Kanamori Katsuhiro]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[Kan'ei]] era ([[1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[Keian]] 3 ([[1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hida-Takayama lords]] [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:Kanamori clan]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]] [[Category:飛騨高山藩主]]

Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]] [[Category:飛騨高山藩主]]

In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]] [[Category:飛騨高山藩主]]

For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]] [[Category:飛騨高山藩主]]

{{Citations missing|date=April 2024}} {{Infobox samurai | name=Kanamori Shigeyori | image=Kanamori Shigeyori.jpg|200px | image_size=200px | caption=Owned by Ryōgen-in | period=[[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]] - [[Edo period]] early | birth=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) | death=[[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]]) | other_names=(Childhood name) Sahei | posthumous_name=Shinryūinden Zuion Sōshō Daikoji | burial=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku]], Murasakino, [[Daitoku-ji]] | court_Rank=[[Junior Fifth Rank|Jushii]]; [[Nagato Province|Nagato no Kami]]; [[Izumo Province|Izumo no Kami]] | shogunate=[[Edo shogunate]] | master=[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada|Hidetada]] → [[Tokugawa Iemitsu|Iemitsu]] | domain=[[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]] lord | clan=[[Kanamori clan]] | parents=Father: [[Kanamori Katsuhiro]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:17世紀の歌人]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:金森飛騨守家]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]]

Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]]

{{Citations missing|date=April 2024}} {{Infobox samurai | name=Kanamori Shigeyori | image=Kanamori Shigeyori.jpg|200px | image_size=200px | caption=Owned by Ryōgen-in | period=[[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]] - [[Edo period]] early | birth=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) | death=[[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]]) | other_names=(Childhood name) Sahei | posthumous_name=Shinryūinden Zuion Sōshō Daikoji | burial=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku]], Murasakino, [[Daitoku-ji]] | court_Rank=[[Junior Fifth Rank|Jushii]]; [[Nagato Province|Nagato no Kami]]; [[Izumo Province|Izumo no Kami]] | shogunate=[[Edo shogunate]] | master=[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|Hidetada]] → [[Tokugawa Iemitsu|Iemitsu]] | domain=[[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]] lord | clan=[[Kanamori clan]] | parents=Father: [[Kanamori Katsuhiro]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:江戸時代の茶人]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647294]] Wikidata name: Kanamori Shigeyori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647294]] Wikidata name: Kanamori Shigeyori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations missing|date=April 2024}} {{Infobox samurai | name=Kanamori Shigeyori | image=Kanamori Shigeyori.jpg|200px | image_size=200px | caption=Owned by Ryōgen-in | period=[[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]] - [[Edo period]] early | birth=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) | death=[[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]]) | other_names=(Childhood name) Sahei | posthumous_name=Shinryūinden Zuion Sōshō Daikoji | burial=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku]], Murasakino, [[Daitoku-ji]] | court_Rank=[[Junior Fifth Rank|Jushii]]; [[Nagato Province|Nagato no Kami]]; [[Izumo Province|Izumo no Kami]] | shogunate=[[Edo shogunate]] | master=[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|Hidetada]] → [[Tokugawa Iemitsu|Iemitsu]] | domain=[[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]] lord | clan=[[Kanamori clan]] | parents=Father: [[Kanamori Katsuhiro]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's su11en death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice pa11ies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s su11en death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigetoshi]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]]

Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigeyori]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]]

Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigeyori]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]]

Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[sn:Kanamori Shigeyori]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}}

Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}}

Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}}

Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Mother: Daughter of [[Nagoya Takahisa]] (sister of [[Nagoya Sansaburō]]; deceased around the time of the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]])
Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Alternative theory: Father: [[Itō Haruaki]]'Dankafu' and 'Kanamori Kafu' include this account. Haruaki was the son of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]]. | siblings=[[Kanamori Shigenari|Shigenari (Sōwa)]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa|Shigemasa]], '''Shigeyori''', [[Kanamori Kanemoto|Kanemoto]], [[Kanamori Shigemasa (Sakyō)|Shigemasa (Sakyō)]], [[Kanamori Shigeyoshi|Shigeyoshi]], [[Sakai Shigezumi]], daughters (wife of [[Matsunaga Hisanobu]]), daughters (wife of an unknown priest), daughters (wives of [[Koide Miyoaki]]) | spouse=Official wife: '''Daughter of [[Naitō Kiyotsugu]]''' | children=[[Kanamori Yorinaga|'''Yorinaga''']], Jūkō, JusumiLater succeeded to the 15th generation of [[Jōdo Shinshū Takayama Betsuin Shōren-ji]] and took the name Senshin., Shigeteru, [[Kanamori Shigenaga|Shigenaga]], HōmyōBecame a 300-koku hatamoto but later went extinct., KatakaBecame a 300-koku hatamoto., ShigetoshiBecame a 3,000-koku hatamoto but died at 18 without heirs., Shigehide
Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金森重頼]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagayasu]]), daughters (wife of [[Oda Nagasada]]), daughters (wife of [[Kamei Koremasa]]), daughters (wife of [[Kinoshita Toshisada]]), daughters (wife of [[Koide Ariyuki]]) | notes= }} '''Kanamori Shigeyori''' (金森 重頼, [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 1 ([[:en:1596|1596]]) – [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 [[October 7 (lunar calendar)|Intercalary October 7]] ([[November 30, 1650]])) was the third lord of the [[:en:Hida-Takayama Domain|Hida-Takayama Domain]]. == Life == Shigeyori served [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] from a young age and was appointed Nagato no Kami in Keichō 18 ([[:en:1614|1614]]). During the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], he served directly under Ieyasu rather than in his father Katsuhiro’s forces. Following Katsuhiro's sudden death in June of that year, Shigeyori inherited the family domain in July by Ieyasu's decree, bypassing his elder brothers, Shigenari (Kanamori Sōwa) and Shigemasa, who also served the [[:en:Tokugawa Hidetada|second shogun]]. Shigeyori focused on governance, continuing his grandfather's and father's efforts to develop the domain. He invested in new rice paddies and silver mines, earning a reputation as a wise ruler. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 1 ([[:en:1615|1615]]), he complied with the [[One Castle per Province Act]] by decommissioning [[Masushima Castle]] and [[Hagiwara Suwa Castle]], converting them into “inns” for official travel. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4 ([[:en:1618|1618]]), he was assigned custody of [[Matsudaira Tadateru]], and in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 ([[:en:1626|1626]]), custody was transferred to [[Suwano Katsumizu]]. During the famine of the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] era ([[:en:1640s|1640s]]), he sold the family treasure, the tea jar [[Unzan Katatsugi]], to [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]] lord [[Kyōgoku Takahiro]] for 3,000 ryō to aid his retainers and farmers. This act cemented his reputation for benevolence. A connoisseur of [[:en:Tea ceremony|Tea ceremony]], poetry, and the arts, Shigeyori also established kilns in his domain. He passed away in [[:en:Keian|Keian]] 3 ([[:en:1650|1650]]) at the age of 55, succeeded by his son, [[Kanamori Yorinaga|Yorinaga]]. Four retainers committed junshi (ritual suicide) following his death. == Family Inheritance == The reasons for Shigeyori’s inheritance are unclear. Possible factors include discord between Katsuhiro and his eldest son Shigenari, and disputes between the mothers of Shigenari and Shigeyori. Another theory suggests Shigeyori was the son of [[Itō Haruaki]], making him a direct descendant of [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], unlike his siblings. Katsuhiro’s sudden death remains unexplained. == Relatives == The Kanamori clan had longstanding marriage ties with the [[Koide clan]] of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s family. For example, [[Koide Yoshimasa]] married Shigeyori's sister, linking the Kanamori family to the lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. Other Kanamori daughters also married into prominent families. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * ''Gifu Prefecture History: Early Modern Volume,'' Gifu Prefecture, 1968, pp. 538–540. {{Hida-Takayama lords|3rd|1615–1650}} {{translated page|ja|金森重頼|version=100065529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:金森重頼]] {{wikidata link|Q11647294}} [[Category:1596 births]] [[Category:1650 deaths]] [[Category:17th-century poets]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kanamori Hidanokami Family]] [[Category:Lords of Hida-Takayama Domain]] [[Category:Poets of the 17th Century]] [[Category:Tea masters of the Edo period]] [[Category:Waka poets of the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
